items.title: Teenagers brandish knives, hammer at Canberra shopping centre

children.content: Police say they were called to the Canberra Centre about 6:20pm on Friday after reports that a group of young people were involved in a disturbance, brandishing knives and a hammer.

image.altText: A police car with its lights on outside a shopping centre.

items.title: Driver sentenced to 13 years' jail over hit-and-run of Canberra schoolboys

children.content: It's almost a year to the day since Tayler Hazell drove a stolen car erratically through Canberra, seriously injuring two children.

image.altText: Three police officers stand talking seriously next to a road with cones on it.

items.title: End of an era for general store owned by family for 100 years

children.content: The Tharwa General Store — which has been run by the same family since 1926 — has been many things: a post office, a mechanic's workshop, a fuel stop, a lolly shop, a watering hole, and above all, a place where a community comes together.

gallery.altText: A black and white image of a woman reaching for something behind the counter of a regional general store.
The exterior of a weathered regional general store with two dilapidated petrol pumps out the front.

thumbnail.altText: A black and white image of a woman reaching for something behind the counter of a regional general store.
The exterior of a weathered regional general store with two dilapidated petrol pumps out the front.

items.title: 'Gigantic waves' batter NSW coastline amid 'treacherous' surf conditions

children.content: Huge swells have inundated NSW beaches as most of the coastline remains closed to the public due to hazardous surf conditions.

image.altText: The Sandon Point Surf Life Saving Club building with large waves seen in the distance.

items.title: 'Electrons to the people:' Neighbours build commercial solar farm

children.content: Hundreds of residents of a rural NSW town combine to build their own commercial solar farm, demonstrating the community benefits of renewable energy.

image.altText: Family of six sitting and smailing amongst a solar farm

items.title: Fuel costs drive COVID-like surge in demand for emergency relief

children.content: Rising petrol and diesel prices are driving Canberra families to seek emergency assistance and forcing small businesses to consider whether to pass on higher costs to customers.

items.title: Greek villages 'becoming ghost towns' as an alarming problem takes hold

children.content: Greece's population could drop by as much as 20 per cent by 2050, with the government declaring the situation a "national threat".

gallery.altText: Klairi standing with the Acropolis in the background.

thumbnail.altText: Klairi standing with the Acropolis in the background.

items.title: Dockers cruise to big win over Tigers as AFL probes Saint's alleged slur

children.content: Fremantle takes charge against Richmond after half-time as St Kilda confirms it is aware of an alleged incident in the VFL involving one of its young players. Follow live.

image.altText: Fremantle players celebrate a goal against Richmond

items.title: Penrith punish Parramatta in brutal first-half blitz

children.content: Penrith have handed their western Sydney rivals a serious reality check with a dominant first half putting them in the box seat against Parramatta. Follow live.

image.altText: Penrith players celebrate scoring a try

items.title: 'A lot of destruction': Thousands remain without power as ex-Cyclone Narelle continues south

children.content: The storm, which has already made landfall in Far North Queensland and the Northern Territory, is now impacting the WA coast.

image.altText: Timelapse of Tropical Cyclone Narelle's path

articles.title: WA gas facilities, ports suffer major disruptions after cyclone

children.content: Gas giants Woodside, Santos and Chevron confirm they have suffered production outages at its gas as a result of ex-Tropical Cyclone Narelle.

image.altText: An aerial shot of the Gorgon gas project on the coast of Western Australia.

articles.title: 'Like a war zone': Exmouth picks up the pieces in wake of ex-Cyclone Narelle

children.content: Ex-Tropical Cyclone Narelle weakens as it travels south through WA's Wheatbelt, leaving a trail of destruction in its wake.

items.title: Tiger Woods arrested following car crash near Florida home

children.content: Golf legend Tiger Woods is arrested in Florida after refusing to submit a urine sample following a car crash near his Florida home.

image.altText: Golfer Tiger Woods stands by his overturned vehicle

items.title: This man went viral after surviving Bondi. Then the internet took a dark turn

children.content: Arsen Ostrovsky was on the ground, bleeding, at the Bondi attack when he decided to take a selfie. Within 2 hours it was circulating on the internet. In less than a day, it had become fodder for a global conspiracy theory.

image.altText: A middle-aged man with a white bandage on his head. Telegram messages and images fade in behind him.

items.title: Critical bridges across Lebanon's Litani River targeted in calculated invasion plan

children.content: Israel's expanded invasion of southern Lebanon was at least 12 days in the making, with bridges targeted to effectively cut off the south from the country's centre.

image.altText: thumbnail IDF Dlafy

articles.title: US 'on verge' of ending Iran war as Rubio demands allies lead postwar coalition

children.content: Secretary of State Marco Rubio tells G7 leaders to ready a postwar coalition to secure the Strait of Hormuz, saying the US-Israeli war on Iran could conclude within weeks.

image.altText: Marco Rubio addresses reporters on the tarmac infront of a plane.

items.title: Palestinian-Australian author Randa Abdel-Fattah speaks at NSW writers' festival

children.content: Her inclusion at the event was slammed by NSW Premier Chris Minns, but Dr Abdel-Fattah told an audience in Newcastle today that art and literature can be spaces for political expression.

image.altText: an author, holding a microphone, speaking to an audience

items.title: 'Built on deception': How one online travel site is fleecing customers

children.content: As holiday-makers gear up for Easter, online travel site Guest Reservations is accused of fleecing customers using hidden fees and charges with advocates calling on government and the regulator to step in.

image.altText: A man wearing a suit pulls a suitcase through an airport.

items.title: The nine books that knocked our critics' socks off this month

children.content: Wondering what to read next? Gothic crime fiction, a cult Japanese bestseller and a fantastical twist on the Blitz are among our critics' latest recommendations.

items.title: Newlyweds sell everything to buy 160-year-old former pub

children.content: A 160-year-old hotel sat on the market for four years, until Jason and Kyla Romaior decided to take a risk. Insurance costs are $20,000 a year, but they are confident they have what it takes to handle the challenges of owning a heritage building.

image.altText: couple leaning over fence on verandah of wooden building, smiling

children.content: As music festivals collapse, one Geelong kid's hobby has turned into a big day out.

items.title: Head of TGA speaks out on sunscreen scandal

children.content: In his first interview since the sunscreen controversy erupted almost 10 months ago, the Therapeutic Goods Administration head says he hopes the proposed shake-up of regulations will restore consumer confidence.

items.title: Trump's new tourism envoy once proposed banishing pigeons from Sydney streets

children.content: US President Donald Trump has named Nick Adams as his new special presidential envoy for tourism. It isn't the Australian's first time in government.

image.altText: Nick Adams and Donald Trump stand in front of American flags.

items.title: Remote WA area connected to outside world with first at-home internet service

children.content: Residents from remote Ngalingkadji in Western Australia's north are being connected to the internet at home for the first time, bringing banking services, telehealth and emergency services.

children.content: Australia power to a 103-run win over the West Indies, despite Stafanie Taylor's breakthrough one-day century.

items.title: Do we actually have the workforce for the $4b NDIS alternative?

items.title: Isolated school students make sacrifices amid fuel crisis

children.content: Ruben Driver has a round trip of 4,000 kilometres to reach his boarding school. The cost of fuel is placing his family under immense pressure to get him to class.

items.title: Is government intervention keeping LNG exporters on their 'best behaviour'?

children.content: With war in the Middle East throwing energy markets into chaos, Australian gas prices are strikingly subdued. Experts say looming intervention by Canberra may explain why.

image.altText: A ship docked in the ocean near Darwin.

children.content: Showmen are determined to keep putting on agricultural shows for regional Australia despite the "frightening" cost of fuel.

image.altText: A man and a woman stand infront of a Dagwood dog stand at a show.

items.title: Ranked number one on the terrorism index, this nation struck back

children.content: As global attention remains fixed on the sprawling war in the Middle East, another conflict is edging towards a dangerous threshold — one between nuclear-armed Pakistan and Taliban-controlled Afghanistan.

image.altText: A Taliban soldier operates an anti-aircraft gun on a clear day.

items.title: EPA, developer defend records over contentious WA beach development

children.content: The City of Busselton says the watchdog has ignored its own rules after giving the Smiths Beach development the green light, but Fini Group says its work was "rigorous".

items.title: Dementia is Australia's top killer, yet most still miss the early signs

children.content: Dementia is the leading cause of death in Australia and stigmas around the disease can delay diagnosis. But some in the community are at work to break taboos.

image.altText: A close-up shot of a hand resting on an elderly woman's hands.

children.content: A rising Queensland runner is drawing national attention after dominating three events — but said his dream sport lies elsewhere.

items.title: How does it feel to be stung by one of the world's most venomous jellyfish?

children.content: A tiny jellyfish found in Queensland has venom so powerful it causes feelings of "impending doom".
